Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Thomas, thanks for updating the patch.

Is there any chance you could make a simple conf-sanity.sh test for this command, so that it at least basic functionality is verified (i.e. "lshowmount" on the MDS and OSS will list the clients from $CLIENTS, or similar).

(6 comments)

Mark -1 until Test-Parameters: line is removed from commit comment so that the test is actually run, along with addressing the params issues James mentioned, otherwise this won't work on upstream kernels.

Patch Set 6:

Tests uploaded by Shadow Autotest from shadow-12vm1 for review-dne-part-1 received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.7/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/ee6a3082-cc4f-11e5-b519-5254006e85c2). Ran 7 tests.  1 tests failed: conf-sanity.

Patch Set 6:

As suspected, the new conf-sanity test fails for systems with NIDs not 0@lo.

Thomas, could you please update the test so that it is more generic.  It is probably best to check for NIDs that match one of those reported by "lctl list_nids" on the server nodes.

Patch Set 6:

(8 comments)

Moving comments forward from v6 so they are not lost.

Unforunately, this patch was tracked under a closed ticket and did not get any attention for the 2.9.0 release.  I've created a new LU-8599 ticket for tracking the patch so that it can hopefully get some attention.

Patch Set 7:

(2 comments)

conf-sanity failed with:

 lshowmount: command not found

Is the "lshowmount" command listed in the .spec file?  It should be part of the "lustre" RPM, but not for client-only builds.

The second failure was:

 mount.lustre: mount /dev/mapper/lvm--Role_OSS-P1 at /mnt/lustre-ost1 failed: Address already in use
 The target service's index is already in use. (/dev/mapper/lvm--Role_OSS-P1)

It looks like the second problem is caused by test_99 not cleaning up ost1 at the end of the test?
Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

parents.subject: LU-8629 obdclass: Fix error exit loop in cl_env_percpu_init

commit.subject: LU-8599 utils: restore lshowmount utility
commit.message:
LU-8599 utils: restore lshowmount utility

The lshowmount utility was removed in commit b5a7260ae8f as being
obsolete, but it was not as unused as previously thought.

Restore lshowmount.c, lshowmount.8, nidlist.c, and nidlist.h  from
history, with minor updates to the Makefiles and .gitignore to avoid
conflicts in context.
